title: Java APIs: Objects, I/O, Collections, Concurrency

questions.question: What does "Java".length() return?
questions.explanation: length() counts char units: J-a-v-a = 4.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: String literals may be interned in the pool, so == can be true for same literal content.

questions.question: String.equals compares:
questions.explanation: String.equals compares content; == compares references.

questions.question: What does new StringBuilder("ab").append("c").toString() yield?
questions.explanation: StringBuilder mutates then toString returns abc.

questions.question: Which is unchecked?
questions.explanation: NullPointerException extends RuntimeException.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: Exception caught prints A; finally always runs printing B.

questions.question: try-with-resources requires resources to implement:
questions.explanation: AutoCloseable enables automatic close.

questions.question: java.nio.file.Path is obtained commonly via:
questions.explanation: NIO Path factories are Paths.get / Path.of.

questions.question: Reading all lines from a text file in modern Java often uses:
questions.explanation: Files.readAllLines reads entire file into List<String>.

questions.question: List<String> list = new ArrayList<>(); uses:
questions.explanation: <> lets compiler infer ArrayList<String> from left-hand side.

questions.question: Which compiles?
options: List<? extends Number> n = new ArrayList<Integer>();
questions.explanation: extends wildcard allows assignment from ArrayList<Integer>; others fail.

questions.question: HashSet iteration order is:
questions.explanation: HashSet does not maintain insertion order.

questions.question: What does Map.of("a", 1, "b", 2).get("a") return?
questions.explanation: Immutable map factory returns 1 for key a.

questions.question: ArrayList get(int) complexity is:
questions.explanation: Random access by index on ArrayList is constant time.

questions.question: What is printed?
questions.explanation: Stream sum of 1+2+3 is 6.

questions.question: Terminal operations on a Stream:
options: Can be chained after another terminal
Produce a result or side-effect and close the pipeline
questions.explanation: Terminal ops trigger processing; stream should not be reused.

questions.question: Does "cat123".matches("cat\\d+")?
questions.explanation: \d+ means one or more digits; cat123 matches.

questions.question: Pattern.compile("a.b") matches:
options: a followed by any char then b
questions.explanation: . is any char except line terminator by default.

questions.question: Starting a new thread correctly uses:
options: Thread.run() alone for a new OS thread
questions.explanation: start() creates new thread; run() alone runs on current thread.

questions.question: What is a risk of unsynchronized counter++ from multiple threads?
questions.explanation: ++ is read-modify-write not atomic without synchronization.

questions.question: What does String.format("%d", 3.9) do?
questions.explanation: %d expects integral; double 3.9 throws IllegalFormatConversionException.

questions.question: Object.equals default behavior (before override) is:
questions.explanation: Object.equals defaults to reference identity.

questions.question: Can catch(IOException e) appear before catch(Exception e)?
options: No; more specific catch must come first
questions.explanation: Subclass/specific exceptions must appear before general ones.

questions.question: throw vs throws:
options: throw raises an exception; throws declares checked exceptions on method
questions.explanation: throw is statement; throws is method signature clause.

questions.question: BufferedReader is often wrapped around:
questions.explanation: BufferedReader decorates character input readers.

questions.question: Type erasure means at runtime:
options: List<String> and List<Integer> share the same raw List type
questions.explanation: Generic type parameters are erased at runtime.

questions.question: Which collection forbids duplicate elements by Set contract?
options: PriorityQueue always allows duplicates as Set
questions.explanation: Set implementations enforce uniqueness.

questions.question: TreeMap keeps entries:
questions.explanation: Red-black tree orders keys.

questions.question: filter(x -> x % 2 == 0) on [1,2,3,4] then count() equals:
questions.explanation: Even elements 2 and 4 give count 2.

questions.question: parallelStream() may help when:
options: Tasks are large CPU-bound with little shared mutable state
Strict global order required for all side effects
Many threads write one unsynchronized file handle
questions.explanation: Parallel helps sizable CPU work without contention.

questions.question: "aaaa".replaceAll("a+", "b") becomes:
questions.explanation: Greedy a+ matches all a's replaced by single b.

questions.question: synchronized provides:
options: Mutual exclusion and visibility for monitors
questions.explanation: Intrinsic lock excludes concurrent entry and establishes happens-before.

questions.question: ExecutorService.submit(Callable) returns:
questions.explanation: submit yields Future to retrieve result later.

questions.question: catch(IOException | SQLException e) requires:
options: Exceptions in union must be unrelated
No duplicate types; no subclass-superclass pair in union
questions.explanation: Multi-catch alternatives cannot be related by inheritance.

questions.question: Files.writeString(path, text) (Java 11+) by default encodes as:
questions.explanation: writeString defaults to UTF-8.

questions.question: To add elements to a generic collection parameter, PECS suggests:
questions.explanation: Consumer super: ? super T allows adding T.

questions.question: Queue.poll() on an empty LinkedList used as queue returns:
questions.explanation: poll returns null when empty; element() throws.

questions.question: Collectors.toList() in Java 8 typically produces:
questions.explanation: toList collector yields mutable list implementation.

questions.question: Anchors ^ and $ match:
options: Start and end of line/input (mode dependent)
questions.explanation: ^ start, $ end (MULTILINE affects line vs entire input).

questions.question: volatile ensures:
questions.explanation: volatile establishes visibility; does not make i++ atomic.

questions.question: Comparing strings by content should use:
questions.explanation: equals compares characters; == compares references.

questions.question: Thread.sleep(millis) can throw:
questions.explanation: sleep declares InterruptedException.
